cards.label: Kythik’s
cards.title: Kythik’s
cards.cardSummary: A flexible route for real runs: take the early chest, chase purple+ value, use ? tiles, and avoid wasting late chest slots.
cards.profile: This is the default route. It follows the way Kythik plays the board: take the early chest unless a clearly better line is already visible, then build value through purple+ chests, strong upgrade cards, ? tiles, and smart movement-card use.
cards.useWhen: Use this when you want the safest all-around plan that is easy to follow during a run.
cards.avoidWhen: Switch away only when you want a tighter max-value route or a clear 7-chest completion route.

walkthrough.heading: Early board
steps: Take the early chest by default.
Skip it only if a better chest or orange/red upgrade card is already reachable with the cards in hand.
Do not gamble on a line that needs a lucky future card.

walkthrough.heading: Main priority
steps: Purple+ chest tiles come first.
Orange/red upgrade cards are next.
? tiles are strong because they can add chests, upgrade future chests, or improve the board.
Movement-card tiles are worth taking when they keep your route alive.
Green/blue chests are fine when efficient, but do not hard-path them.

walkthrough.heading: Late board
steps: Around 5 chests, slow down on green/blue chests.
Try to keep 1–2 chest slots open if there is still a lot of board left.
Those open slots can turn into better loot if ? tiles upgrade or add future chests.

notes: Best practical default for normal play.
Value first, but do not throw away chest slots late.
Strong average value with better 7-chest completion than the older weight-only control.

cards.label: Math Says Best
cards.title: Math Says Best
cards.cardSummary: Highest tested value. Preserves movement cards harder and avoids spending cards unless the payoff is clearly worth it.
cards.profile: This route scored the highest in testing. It plays tighter than the default route: movement cards are protected, weak refill lines are ignored, and cards are spent only when the board gives a real payoff.
cards.useWhen: Use this when you can slow down, compare routes, and avoid spending movement cards just because they are available.
cards.avoidWhen: Avoid this when you want a simpler route or when you are likely to miss multi-step lines under pressure.

walkthrough.heading: Core idea
steps: Do not spend movement cards casually.
A movement card should buy real value: purple+ chest access, a strong upgrade card, a valuable ? tile, or a safer future route.
Passing on a small pickup is often correct if it keeps your hand strong.

walkthrough.heading: What to take
steps: Take purple+ chests when the route is reasonable.
Take orange/red upgrade cards when they can improve current or future chests.
Take ? tiles when they are reachable without draining your hand.
Take green/blue chests mostly when they are cheap or late.

walkthrough.heading: Common mistake
steps: Do not use a card just to reach one movement-card tile.
Do not chase a small chest if it leaves you with no route tools.
This route wins by having options later.

notes: Requires tighter card discipline than the default route.
Protect movement cards unless the payoff is real.

cards.label: 7 Chests → Rarity
cards.title: 7 Chests → Rarity
cards.cardSummary: A clear completion route: get near 7 chests, reserve late slots, use ? tiles and upgrades, then finish the last chest slots late.
cards.profile: This route is for players who want a clearer chest-completion goal. It does not mean click every chest as soon as possible. The plan is to build toward 6 chests, keep space open while the board still has value left, then finish the last slot later.
cards.useWhen: Use this when your main goal is finishing all 7 chest slots without filling them too early with weak chests.
cards.avoidWhen: Avoid this when you only care about highest average value or when the board already gives an obvious premium route.

walkthrough.heading: Build toward 6
steps: Take efficient early chests.
Prioritize purple+ chests and strong upgrade cards.
Use ? tiles because they can add or upgrade future chests.
Do not ignore movement-card tiles if they keep the route flexible.

walkthrough.heading: Reserve the last slot
steps: At 5–6 chests, stop rushing green/blue chests if a lot of board remains.
Leave 1–2 chest slots open so future ? effects can create or improve better chests.
Purple+ chests are still worth taking; the warning is mostly about weak chests.

walkthrough.heading: Finish late
steps: Once the board is running out, take the chest that finishes 7.
A late green/blue chest is fine if it completes the board.
The mistake is filling the final slot too early, not taking it late.

notes: Best completion-focused route from the nuanced test.
Get close to 7, keep slots open, then finish late.
This is not a click-every-chest strategy.
